data.description:
# P1.4 (rev) — Bounded git growth via stock gc + non-destructive (union) recovery

> Supersedes `seek-algae-reign` (a240-d692-a86f-411c). The original epic proposed a
> bespoke operator `rebar gc` with hand-tuned reflog/prune windows to protect a
> reflog-only recovery net. Critical evaluation + 7 git experiments + two Opus
> reviews (principal-engineer + peer-comparison) showed that design is an outlier:
> no comparable tool builds it, and it treats a symptom (a recovery path that
> orphans commits into the reflog) instead of the cause. This epic implements the
> peer-aligned design instead. Full design trail: session_log (see relates_to).

## Context (problem + why the rewrite)

Every ticket mutation is a committed file on the orphan `tickets` branch. The write
path forces `gc.auto=0` (`_store/event_append.py:74-84 _ensure_gc_auto_zero`, also
`compact.py:61`, `txn.py:217/413`, `init.py:182`) for ONE reason: to keep git's
reflog alive, because `_store/sync.py` `_do_reconverge` does
`git reset --hard origin/tickets` in the "unrelated histories" path (~71-85), which
**orphans local-only commits** and relies on the reflog to recover them (the
warning at `sync.py:81` literally says "recoverable: git reflog"). No durable ref
is saved before the reset. Cost: unbounded loose-object/pack growth with no safe
reclaim, and a recovery net that depends on an ephemeral, gc-governed structure
the operator cannot see.

## Validated facts (our experiments — see session_log)

- Reachable commits survive even `git gc --prune=now`; only `reset --hard` orphans
  + reflog-expiry can lose data (EXP-A/B).
- On the append-only branch `git gc` is ~96% repack/delta (2.5M -> 104K, 600 objects
  intact) and `--prune` reclaims ~nothing; "deleted"/compacted blobs stay reachable
  from history so `--compact-first` reclaims nothing for gc (EXP-E/G).
- `git gc` produces cruft packs by default on git >= 2.41 (EXP-F).
- A durable ref makes an orphaned commit survive a FULL reflog wipe + `gc --prune=now`
  (EXP-H) — i.e. reachability, not the reflog, is the durable guarantee.

## Prior art (verifiable references — the patterns we follow)

- **git-bug** — leaves `gc.auto` on, ships NO gc command, recovers by DAG **union
  merge (fast-forward-only, both parents kept), never `reset --hard`**; batches many
  ops into one blob/commit (operationPack):
  https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/entity_actions.go ,
  https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/blob/master/entity/dag/operation_pack.go
- **git-appraise** — git-notes unioned via `cat_sort_uniq` (never drops); before its
  one rewrite path it **archives the old head to a ref "to keep history from being
  garbage collected"** (ref-reachability, not reflog):
  https://github.com/google/git-appraise/blob/master/repository/git.go ,
  https://github.com/google/git-appraise/blob/master/review/review.go
- **git-issue** — one-commit-per-change, stock gc, no bespoke maintenance:
  https://github.com/dspinellis/git-issue
- **jujutsu (jj)** — recovery net is the op log materialized as real `refs/jj/keep/`
  refs; gc (`jj util gc`) is safe because it only collects objects unreachable from
  retained refs => "gc reachability == recovery guarantee" co-design:
  https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/operation-log/ ,
  https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/technical/architecture/
- **git itself** — `gc.auto` default (6700 loose) is the normal posture; cruft packs
  default since 2.37/2.41; `--prune=now` risks corruption under concurrent writers;
  `git maintenance` is the scale-up scheduler (disabling gc.auto WITHOUT out-of-band
  maintenance is the anti-pattern, cf. Scalar/GitLab):
  https://git-scm.com/docs/git-gc , https://git-scm.com/docs/git-maintenance ,
  https://github.blog/open-source/git/highlights-from-git-2-37/ ,
  https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/introducing-scalar/

## Design + the safety invariant

Adopt the universal peer pattern: **trust stock `git gc`, and make recovery
non-destructive so the reflog is never load-bearing.**

> INVARIANT: after union recovery, every commit rebar cares about is ref-reachable;
> therefore stock `git gc` is safe by construction — it only ever collects truly
> unreachable objects.

This is jj's co-design lesson achieved for free: if commits are never orphaned,
gc has nothing unsafe to collect.

## Implementation plan (work units)

- **WU-1 Re-enable stock gc.** Delete `_ensure_gc_auto_zero` (`event_append.py:74-84`)
  + its call (`:124`); drop the four `git config gc.auto 0` writes (`txn.py:217`,
  `txn.py:413`, `compact.py:61`, `init.py:182`). Set `gc.autoDetach=true` explicitly
  at init so a forked background gc never serializes a foreground write. Migration:
  idempotent `git config --unset gc.auto || true` at init/fsck.
- **WU-2 Union, don't discard.** Replace the `reset --hard origin/tickets` in
  `sync.py` Path 1 (unrelated histories) with
  `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories origin/tickets --no-edit`, **reusing the
  existing diverged-Path-3 conflict -> `merge --abort` -> keep-local -> hint
  handling** (extend proven code, do not invent). Keep `reset --hard` only for the
  ff-adopt path (discards nothing).
- **WU-3 Conflict policy for mutable shared root files.** uuid-named ticket dirs
  never collide, but `.bridge_state/bindings.json` and the reconciler lock/gate
  files are rewritten per pass and would conflict. Add a tickets-branch
  `.gitattributes` with `merge=ours` for `.bridge_state/*` and `.reconciler-*`
  (the loser is a derived cache the reconciler rebuilds next pass — never ticket
  events). `merge=union` is WRONG (line-unions JSON into invalid JSON). NOTE:
  push.py already handles these conflicts today, so this only formalizes it.
- **WU-4 Docs + invariant.** Rewrite `docs/concurrency.md:165-196` and the `sync.py`
  module docstring (both assert the old reset+reflog design). State the safety
  invariant; note `git maintenance run` as the scale-up posture and that git's own
  30d-unreachable reflog window remains as a free backstop we no longer depend on.

## What this supersedes / drops from the old epic

- DROP the bespoke `rebar gc` command, `reflog expire --expire=<14d>`, the 14-day
  window rationale, `--compact-first` (reclaims nothing — EXP-G), and the
  `check_no_rebase_in_progress`-as-gc-guard (recovery is `reset --hard`, atomic, no
  marker — the guard cannot see it). None of this machinery exists in `src/` yet;
  this epic ensures it never gets built.

## Success Criteria

- `gc.auto` is re-enabled; stock background `git gc` reclaims loose/pack growth with
  no bespoke command and no data loss (a `gc --prune=now` regression loses no
  reachable ticket data).
- Unrelated-history sync MERGES (never discards) local tickets; two clones each
  rewriting `bindings.json` converge without a wedge.
- Docs assert the safety invariant; the reflog is no longer load-bearing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WU-1: `_ensure_gc_auto_zero` + all four `gc.auto 0` writes removed; `gc.autoDetach=true` set at init; idempotent `--unset gc.auto` migration
- [ ] WU-2: `sync.py` Path 1 uses `git merge --allow-unrelated-histories` (reusing Path 3 conflict handling); ff-adopt path unchanged; no `reset --hard` that orphans local commits remains
- [ ] WU-3: tickets-branch `.gitattributes` with `merge=ours` for `.bridge_state/*` and `.reconciler-*`; convergence test for two clones racing `bindings.json`
- [ ] WU-4: `docs/concurrency.md` + `sync.py` docstring rewritten to the invariant; `git maintenance` noted as scale-up
- [ ] Tests: union-recovery loses no tickets; `gc --prune=now` loses no reachable data; bindings.json race converges
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green
data.title: P1.4 (rev) — Bounded git growth via stock gc + union recovery
